name: Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail
name_original: Running circular por el Monte de las Cenizas
description: Jog the 4.8-mile Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail, a moderate route with 885 feet of gain, offering historical sites and panoramic sea views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Unión offer diverse terrain, from coastal paths to mountainous areas. The region is characterized by its varied landscape, including the foothills of the Cordillera and areas with historical mining features. These natural features provide a range of environments for running, from moderate trails to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around La Unión The most popular jogging route is Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 59 minutes to complete, offering moderate elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Old Mining Factory – Miner's Monument loop from La Unión, a difficult 13.3 miles (21.4 km) path through historical mining landscapes. Local runners also love the Cabezo Las Lajas loop from Sierra Minera, a 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra Minera, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Jogging around La Unión is defined by coastal paths, hills, and historical mining areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult mountain runs. The routes in La Unión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore La Unión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Unión?
A: La Unión offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 20 trails to explore. These routes cater to different fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in La Unión?
A: Jogging routes in La Unión feature diverse terrain, ranging from coastal paths with refreshing sea breezes to more challenging trails through the foothills of the Cordillera and historical mining landscapes. You'll find a mix of paved sections and natural paths.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in La Unión?
A: Yes, La Unión has several easy running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed pace. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the region offers options that are less demanding in terms of elevation and distance.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in La Unión?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners, La Unión offers challenging trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33731725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Mining Factory – Miner's Monument loop from La Unión&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 13.3-mile (21.4 km) path through historical mining areas, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27642303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo Las Lajas loop from Sierra Minera&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.5-mile (18.5 km) trail through the Sierra Minera.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see while running in La Unión?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33714694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Peña del Águila Trail loop from La Unión Vieja&lt;/a&gt; provides coastal vistas. You can also explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7211523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas and Peña del Águila Regional Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a natural monument, or enjoy the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/199520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Unión?
A: Yes, many of the popular running routes in La Unión are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900956768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21854725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Unión Vieja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Unión?
A: The running routes in La Unión are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 300 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to historical mining areas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, La Unión's history, particularly its mining heritage, is evident along many trails. You can encounter historical mining landscapes on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33731725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Mining Factory – Miner's Monument loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molinete Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3794391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by water features like lakes or wetlands?
A: While the region is known for its coastline, some routes may offer proximity to other water features. Nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5012560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Mar Menor&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6299230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saladar de lo Poyo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3939532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marina del Carmolí Wetlands&lt;/a&gt;, which are significant natural areas in the vicinity.

Q: What is the longest running route available in La Unión?
A: For those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33714694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Peña del Águila Trail loop from La Unión Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, covering approximately 17.2 miles (27.7 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any running trails that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, La Unión's coastline provides extensive stretches for running with refreshing sea breezes and scenic views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33714694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Portman Bay – Peña del Águila Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful coastal vistas, and you can also find runs near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/199520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain. You'll jog through a winding forest track with Aleppo pines, holm oaks, and other Mediterranean flora. Be prepared for sections with an uneven cobblestone surface (about 1400 meters) and a rocky track. There are also increasingly steep ascents towards the fortifications. A unique feature is the presence of an ancient Roman road, parts of which are very well preserved.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally considered easy to moderate for walking, the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail can be moderately challenging for jogging, especially for beginners. The route includes increasingly steep sections and uneven surfaces like cobblestone and rocky tracks. A fit jogger can navigate it, but you might need to reduce your pace or walk during climbs or over rougher ground.

faqs.question: What historical features and viewpoints can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in history and offers stunning views. You'll encounter impressive British-built Vickers and Armstrong 38.1 cm guns from 1931, housed in a grand Mayan-style structure. Along the way, you can explore ruins of other fortifications and walk on an ancient Roman road. The Batería de las Cenizas Viewpoint provides magnificent panoramic views of the Bay of Portmán, Mar Menor, and the entire coast of Calblanque, including vistas of La Manga and the cliffs of Sierra de la Fausilla.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to visit the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making jogging less comfortable. Spring also offers the advantage of seeing the Mediterranean flora in bloom.

faqs.question: Is the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Natural Park of Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail or the Natural Park of Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila for general recreational use like jogging or hiking. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for group activities or specific events.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park for the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is usually available near the trailhead, often close to the entrance of the Natural Park or specific access points for the Monte de las Cenizas area. Look for designated parking areas to ensure you're not obstructing local traffic or protected zones. Specific parking details can often be found on local tourism websites or detailed maps of the park.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte de las Cenizas Loop Trail intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the route overlap with the Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia (48%) and the Monte Cenizas trail (42%). You'll also find sections that are part of the Llano del Beal (29%) and the ancient Calzada Romana (23%).

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated in an area of great ecological value, showcasing diverse Mediterranean flora. You'll pass through dense pine forests with Aleppo pines, holm oaks, Cartagena cypresses, mastic trees, blackthorns, rosemary, and palm trees. As for fauna, keep an eye out for various bird species and small mammals typical of the Mediterranean scrubland, though they can be elusive.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Portman, which offers some basic amenities. For a wider selection of cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation, you might need to venture a bit further to larger towns or coastal areas nearby. It's advisable to bring your own water and snacks, as options directly at the trailhead are limited.
